title: List all callers of lookupCommand
prompt: In this C codebase, find EVERY function that calls `lookupCommand`. Give me the final answer as a plain list of caller function names, one per line, under a line that says 'CALLERS:'. Be exhaustive but precise (real call sites only, not declarations/comments/strings).

title: Who triggers wpa_driver_wext_scan to run (no-build)
prompt: In this C codebase, I need to change the function `wpa_driver_wext_scan`. Which functions in the codebase would actually cause it to be executed at runtime (its effective callers)? List the function names, one per line, under a line that says 'CALLERS:'.

title: Transitive blast radius of lookupCommand (depth 2)
prompt: In this C codebase, compute the transitive set of functions that would be affected if I change `lookupCommand` — i.e. its callers, and their callers, up to 2 levels deep. Give the final answer as a plain list of function names, one per line, under a line that says 'AFFECTED:'.

title: Explain processCommand (callers + callees)
prompt: In this C codebase, explain the function `processCommand`: what it does, which functions call it, and which functions it calls. Give the final answer with three labelled sections 'SUMMARY:', 'CALLERS:', 'CALLEES:' (names one per line under the latter two).

title: Explain hostapd_driver_scan relationships (no-build)
prompt: In this C codebase (no build system / no compile_commands.json), explain the function `hostapd_driver_scan`: which functions call it and which functions it calls (including any function-pointer dispatch). Give the final answer with sections 'CALLERS:' and 'CALLEES:' (names one per line).
